violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employee(s) must successfully complete an approved course in food safety management. One employee must then register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager. Application provided. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2026

Meatballs, prepared yesterday, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Meatballs observed with a temperature of 45 degrees F. Voluntary disposal during inspection. Proper cooling reviewed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026

Deli meats observed not marked with the date of preparation (slicing). Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
Spray bottles used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Meatballs were placed hot in the walk-in cooler in a deep container. Proper cooling methods reviewed and resource provided. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
Knives observed stored between the knife rack and the wall. Food equipment/utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2026
Spray bottle used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies was not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2025
Knives observed to be stored between the knife rack and the wall. Utensils must be stored in a clean location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/26/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2025
